channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Texans never trail while handing Cowboys 5th straight loss, 34-10
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Joe Mixon ran for three touchdowns, Derek Barnett returned a fumble 28 yards for a score and the Houston Texans beat the Dallas Cowboys 34-10 on Monday night. The Texans (7-4) stopped just the second two-game losing streak in quarterback C.J.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pulisic scores twice, US beats Jamaica 4-2 for 5-2 aggregate win in CONCACAF Nations League QF
item.description: Christian Pulisic scored twice in the first half, Tim Weah got a goal in his return to the U.S. national team from a red card and the Americans beat Jamaica 4-2 for a 5-2 aggregate win in their CONCACAF Nations League quarterfinal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Giants bench QB Daniel Jones and will start Tommy DeVito against the Bucs
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (AP) — Daniel Jones' tenure as the New York Giants' starting quarterback is over.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bronny James has a left heel bruise and is listed as doubtful for the Lakers' next game
item.description: Bronny James has a bruised left heel, and he is listed as doubtful for the Los Angeles Lakers’ next game against the Utah Jazz.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NHL referee Mitch Dunning communicative, can move extremities following violent collision
item.description: NHL referee Mitch Dunning is fully communicative and can move all his extremities following a violent collision with Colorado defenseman Josh Manson in Monday night’s game at Philadelphia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: PGA Tour approves reduction to 100 players keeping cards off FedEx Cup list
item.description: The PGA Tour board has approved sweeping changes to eligibility that wipes out 25 cards starting in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Doug Ferguson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pirates ace Paul Skenes and Yankees pitcher Luis Gil win Rookie of the Year awards
item.description: Hard-throwing Pittsburgh Pirates ace Paul Skenes capped a remarkable season by winning the National League Rookie of the Year Award while Luis Gil of the New York Yankees edged Baltimore’s Colton Cowser to take the AL honor.
item.dc:creator: Bernie Wilson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 4-time MLB All-Star Kris Bryant’s Lamborghini recovered after investigation cracked down on car theft ring
item.description: Colorado Rockies star third baseman Kris Bryant had his Lamborghini Huracan stolen as part of a car theft ring while reportedly trying to ship it to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump thinks he can fix golf's mess. He starts by playing golf with PGA Tour commissioner
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump boasted during the campaign he would need about 15 minutes to get a deal done between the PGA Tour and the Saudi investors of LIV Golf. That started with four hours on the golf course with PGA Tour Commissioner Jay Monahan.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump support dominates weekend in sports as athletes perform president-elect's dance moves
item.description: Support for President-elect Donald Trump was on full display across the entire weekend as athletes performed his dance moves during matches and games.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Josh Allen seals Bills' 30-21 win over Chiefs with TD run, ending bid for perfect season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — After watching quarterback Josh Allen bounce off two Kansas City Chiefs defenders before crossing the goal line on a 26-yard run, Bills receiver Khalil Shakir joined the rest of the packed Buffalo stadium by screaming at the top of his lungs.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sengun records triple-double, VanVleet scores 28 in Rockets 143-107 rout of Bulls
item.description: Fred VanVleet scored a season-high 28 points, Alperen Sengun had 20 points, 11 rebounds and 11 assists for his second triple-double this season and the Houston Rockets routed the Chicago Bulls 143-107 on Sunday night for their fifth straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Raiders' Brock Bowers and other NFL players celebrate TDs with Trump-inspired dance moves
item.description: The Trump dance is becoming a trend around the NFL. Raiders tight end Brock Bowers, Titans wide receiver Calvin Ridley and Lions defensive end Za’Darius Smith followed Nick Bosa’s lead and celebrated big plays on Sunday with dance moves inspired by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CMU drops final game at home against Western Colorado
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—After a six-game winning streak, Colorado Mesa University was dealt a setback by South Dakota Mines. Now, the Mavericks return to their home field with the stakes as high as the Rocky Mountains, aiming to secure a playoff spot.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Former Detroit Lions player charged over Capitol riot
item.description: A former Detroit Lions linebacker was arrested and charged with allegedly assaulting law enforcement and other offenses over the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: The world's tallest teenager, 7-foot-9 Olivier Rioux, plans to redshirt at Florida this season
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — The world’s tallest teenager might have to wait a year to become the world’s tallest college basketball player. Florida coach Todd Golden says Olivier Rioux, a 7-foot-9 freshman who owns a spot in the Guinness record book, is planning to redshirt.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blackhawks lose 3-1 in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Jaden Schwartz scored on a power play 1:34 into the third period, and the Seattle Kraken pushed their winning streak to three games with a 3-1 win over the Chicago Blackhawks Thursday night. Matty Beniers and Brandon Tanev also scored.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Vasilevskiy stars as the Lightning stop the Jets' 7-game win streak with a 4-1 victory
item.description: Andrei Vasilevskiy made 24 saves in his 300th NHL win, and the Tampa Bay Lightning stopped Winnipeg’s seven-game win streak with a 4-1 victory over the Jets. Vasilevskiy is the 40th goaltender in league history to win 300 games.
item.dc:creator: JOHN KREISER, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Curry upset after Klay 'ghosted' him before game, Draymond Green says
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- While it seemed to be all sunshine and roses inside Chase Center as the packed arena welcomed back Klay Thompson on Tuesday, that wasn't exactly the case -- at least amongst the players on the court.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Bucs TE Cade Otton emerging as reliable target for Baker Mayfield in absence of injured receivers
item.description: Cade Otton is showing the rest of the NFL the type of offensive weapon the Tampa Bay Buccaneers always felt he could become.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Clippers write love letter to Los Angeles with new City Edition uniforms
item.description: With the NBA now in full swing and the brand-new Intuit Dome open for business, the Los Angeles Clippers have unveiled their City Edition uniforms for the new season. The new look, which the team says symbolizes the story and work of L.A.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: San Diego Gulls to host Military Appreciation Night this Saturday
item.description: Get ready for an exciting evening of hockey, entertainment and heartfelt gratitude this Saturday, Nov. 16, as the San Diego Gulls host their Military Appreciation Night.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Grand Prix of Long Beach sold ahead of iconic race's 50th edition
item.description: The Grand Prix of Long Long Beach, the longest-running major street circuit race in North America, will have a new owner heading into the 50th edition of the event next year. Penske Entertainment announced its purchase of Grand Prix Association of Long Beach, LLC, from longtime owner Gerald R.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Finally healthy, Puka Nacua poised for strong finish
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - Puka Nacua was not supposed to be this good, this quickly. As a fifth round draft pick out of BYU by the L.A. Rams in 2023, Nacua took the NFL by storm, setting rookie records with 104 catches for 1,486 yards.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Collins scores 29, but Jazz fall to Suns, 120-112
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Jazz got big contributions from John Collins and rookie Kyle Filipowski, but it wasn't enough to slow down the Phoenix Suns Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Chicago Bears reconsider old Michael Reese Hospital site as new stadium location
item.description: Chicago Bears are reconsidering the Michael Reese Hospital site as a possible location for a new stadium, as they seek larger private investment and political support for the project.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Interim Oakland Roots head coach gets promoted
item.description: (KRON) – Oakland Roots Soccer Club Interim Head Coach Gavin Glinton can finally ditch the “interim” of his title. The soccer club announced that Glinton has been promoted to full-time Head Coach ahead of the 2025 United Soccer League Championship season.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Dodgers fan claims he was fired for calling in sick, attending World Series parade
item.description: People are willing to go to great lengths to support their beloved sports teams—one person even lost his job for it. A Los Angeles Dodgers fan claims he was fired after being spotted at the team's World Series victory parade after calling in sick for work.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Bucs' Rachaad White celebrates local students’ completion of financial literacy program
item.description: Financial education at an early age can help young people lower debt and increase savings and credit scores as they mature into adulthood, according to the FDIC.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dodger Stadium improvements could provide leg-up in team's pursuit of World Series repeat
item.description: Work has begun at Dodger Stadium, including renovations to the home team clubhouse, which houses team lockers, showers and communal areas.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: John Robinson, successful football coach at USC and with the LA Rams, has died at 89
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — John Robinson, the veteran football coach who enjoyed many years of success at the University of Southern California and with the Los Angeles Rams, has died. He was 89. The Rams confirmed Robinson's death on Monday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5
